outlook.headline: Long-term pricing deals keep broadening across the semiconductor chain
outlook.summary: The read-through is still getting better: the mention trend has climbed sharply to 4.5x, and the latest quarter remains well above the trailing average despite a slight pullback from 4.7x. Momentum is being driven by tighter supply, AI/capacity buildouts, and customers seeking multi-year visibility, which is translating into more locked-in volumes and better pricing stability for suppliers. The main watch-out is that a handful of names still show only generic or weak contract commentary, so breadth is improving but not yet universal.

drivers.label: Tighter supply is forcing longer commitments
drivers.detail: Extremely tight supply and excess molecules are helping suppliers secure multi-year agreements and firmer pricing/volumes.

drivers.label: AI and capacity expansion are locking in demand
drivers.detail: Large compute and fab buildouts are extending customer visibility and supporting longer-term commitments.

drivers.label: More strategic, sticky customer relationships
drivers.detail: Platform-of-record wins, partnerships, and multi-generation supply deals are making agreements more durable and visible.

drivers.label: Some pockets still lack incremental pricing power
drivers.detail: Several companies only discussed partnerships or prior agreements, while one contract read was explicitly weak, limiting breadth.

risks: Supply conditions ease faster than expected, reducing the need for multi-year commitments and price support.
AI/capacity ramp timing slips, which would weaken demand visibility for equipment and infrastructure suppliers.
Weak contract execution at a few names turns into broader caution on long-term agreement quality.
